topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The laptop screen glowed, illuminating the familiar curve of Liam’s jaw. I was just looking for the shared grocery list, but my eyes snagged on the open document. It wasn't a spreadsheet or a work proposal; it was prose. The title, written in elegant script, was simply "The Constant." My breath hitched, a tiny, involuntary catch in my throat. I leaned closer, my heart beginning a frantic drum solo against my ribs. The words detailed my morning routine, the way I hum when I’m concentrating, the precise shade of blue my eyes take in certain lights. A coldness seeped into my limbs, making my fingers tremble slightly as I tried to pull back, but my gaze was magnetically drawn to the narrative unfolding before me. When Liam looked up, sensing my sudden stillness, a flicker of something—surprise, perhaps—crossed his face. The silence stretched, thick and heavy, punctuated only by the faint whir of the cooling fan. I couldn't speak. My mouth felt dry, and I just stared, the reality of what I’d stumbled upon pressing down on my chest until it felt like I couldn't draw a full breath.

[story 2]

I found the file tucked away in a seldom-used folder on his desktop. It was a manuscript, meticulously typed, detailing every awkward glance and unspoken affection we’d ever shared. Reading it felt like watching someone meticulously dissect my soul and repackage it for public consumption. A wave of disorientation washed over me, blurring the edges of the room. I sat back from the desk so abruptly my chair scraped loudly against the hardwood floor. My hands started to move, gripping the edge of the desk until my knuckles were white, a desperate need to physically anchor myself in the present moment. When Liam walked in, whistling tunelessly, he stopped dead. His casual demeanor dissolved instantly. He looked from the screen to my rigid posture, and the air in the apartment seemed to thin out. "What is it?" he asked, his voice unusually low, edged with caution. I managed a small, jerky nod, unable to articulate the dizzying sense of being completely exposed, like a character in someone else’s carefully constructed world.

[story 3]

It started as a casual browsing session, a simple check of his files, but then I saw it. A novel. A novel about me. The sheer audacity of it made my stomach clench. I felt a strange lightness, followed immediately by a crushing weight settling over my shoulders. Every word on the page seemed to vibrate with an intimacy I hadn't realized existed, a depth of observation that felt invasive. I backed away slowly, my movements becoming hesitant, as if navigating a minefield. My vision tunneled slightly; the colors in the room seemed muted, the sounds of the city outside becoming distant static. I finally forced myself to look at him, expecting a defense, an explanation, but instead, I saw only a complicated, unreadable expression. My throat tightened; I swallowed hard, the effort causing a slight tremor in my jaw. I wanted to scream, to demand context, but all that escaped was a barely audible, ragged exhale.
